data.title: Brevo: 7 Tips for Maximizing Your Multi-Channel Marketing
data.excerpt: Discover practical tips and best ways to use Brevo. This email marketing and CRM platform helps you grow with email, SMS, and WhatsApp automation. Try it free!

$media.alt: Brevo: 7 Tips for Maximizing Your Multi-Channel Marketing

children.text: If you're running a growing business, juggling separate tools for email, SMS, customer data, and support chats can feel like spinning plates. That’s where a unified platform shines.
Brevo

brings email, SMS, WhatsApp, chat, and a built-in CRM into one dashboard, and it’s surprisingly accessible. I’ve been exploring its features, and here are the best ways to get the most out of it, whether you're a solopreneur or scaling a team.
This post may contain affiliate links. If you sign up through them, I may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.
Tip 1: Start with the Free Forever Plan (Seriously)
One of Brevo’s standout qualities is its commitment to letting you try before you buy. The
free plan

isn’t a 14-day trial; it’s a permanent tier for individuals building their online presence. You get access to the drag-and-drop editor, basic email templates, and up to 300 emails per day. This is perfect for validating your ideas or nurturing a fledgling list without financial pressure.
Why this matters:

Many platforms gatekeep essential features behind a paywall. Brevo lets you experience the core email marketing tools indefinitely. You can build branded signup forms, use basic segmentation, and track opens and clicks—all on the house. This low barrier to entry is a massive confidence booster for new entrepreneurs.
Pro move:
Use the free plan to set up and test your first automated welcome series. Even with the daily send limit, you can create a powerful sequence that nurtures every new subscriber automatically.
Tip 2: Leverage AI to Draft Campaigns Faster
Staring at a blank subject line or email body can halt a campaign for days. Brevo’s built-in
AI content generator

is a game-changer for productivity. You don’t need to be a copywriter. Just provide a few prompts—like the goal of your email, your product’s key benefit, and your brand voice—and the AI will draft subject lines, full body copy, and even suggestions to adjust the tone.
Concrete example:

For a summer sale announcement, I prompted: “Write a cheerful subject line for a 24-hour flash sale on garden tools for a friendly, family-run store.” The AI generated multiple options like “Sun’s Out, Sale’s On! ⛱️ 24-Hour Garden Tool Deal.” I picked one, tweaked a sentence, and had a campaign ready in minutes, not hours.
Best practice:
Treat the AI output as a skilled first draft. Use its structure and ideas, then infuse your unique voice. This combo of AI speed and human touch produces authentic, high-performing campaigns quickly.
Tip 3: Go Beyond Email with SMS and WhatsApp

Email is powerful, but inboxes are crowded. Brevo’s multi-channel approach lets you meet customers where they’re most responsive. The Starter plan (from just $8.08/month billed annually) includes both email and SMS capabilities. You can send transactional messages, promotional offers, or appointment reminders via text.
The real magic, however, is WhatsApp.

Brevo is an official partner, allowing you to send template messages for order confirmations, shipping updates, and even customer support conversations right inside the platform. Imagine a customer abandoning their cart; an automated WhatsApp message with a personalized discount can recover that sale almost instantly.
Strategic integration:

Use segmentation to decide the right channel. For urgent updates (like a shipping delay), SMS or WhatsApp gets attention. For a weekly newsletter, email is perfect. Brevo’s unified contact history means you see every touchpoint in one profile, so you never over-communicate.
Tip 4: Master Advanced Segmentation for Laser-Focused Messaging
Blasting the same message to everyone is a recipe for high unsubscribe rates. Brevo’s
advanced segmentation

is a feature you should use from day one. You can filter contacts by demographics, website activity (like pages visited), email engagement (opened a specific campaign), or custom events you define (e.g., “downloaded_ebook”).
Powerful use case:

Create a segment of “engaged buyers”: contacts who have purchased in the last 90 days AND opened your last two emails. Send this segment an exclusive early-access offer. Meanwhile, create a re-engagement segment for subscribers who haven’t opened an email in 6 months, and send them a “We miss you” campaign with a compelling reason to come back.
Pro tip:
Save your key segments. Brevo lets you save and name them, so you can reuse them for future campaigns with a single click. This turns a complex data task into a simple workflow.
Tip 5: Use the CRM to Unify Your Sales & Marketing Data

Brevo isn’t just an email tool; it’s a lightweight CRM that connects marketing activity to sales outcomes. Every contact you add has a timeline showing emails opened, forms filled, chats started, and deals they’re associated with. This 360-degree view is invaluable for small teams where the same person might do marketing and sales.
How to apply it:

When a lead fills out a “Contact Sales” form from your Brevo-built landing page, they automatically enter your CRM. You can then assign the deal to a team member, track its stage (New Lead, Contacted, Proposal Sent), and see all prior marketing interactions. This context helps you have more personalized sales conversations.
Keep it tidy:

Regularly clean your CRM lists within Brevo. Use its filters to identify unengaged contacts and move them to a separate, low-frequency list. This keeps your deliverability high and your sales pipeline focused on warm leads.
Who Is Brevo Ideal For?
Brevo is built for
ambitious, budget-conscious businesses
that are tired of patching together five different software subscriptions. It’s particularly perfect for:
Solopreneurs and freelancers
who need to look professional and automate follow-ups without enterprise costs.
Small e-commerce stores
that want to leverage transactional emails (order confirmations), SMS shipping updates, and cart abandonment flows in one place.
Growing startups and agencies
managing multiple client campaigns or communication channels. The ability to add users and manage everything from a single dashboard simplifies operations.
Content creators and educators
nurturing a community with newsletters, course updates, and event reminders across email and WhatsApp.
If your goal is to
systemize your growth
and build deeper customer relationships without drowning in complexity, Brevo is designed for you.
You can start exploring its full potential completely free here.
New & Highlighted Features to Explore
Brevo is consistently evolving. Here are some features that stand out for their impact:
AI Content Generator:
As mentioned, this is a huge time-saver for drafting campaign copy. It’s integrated directly into the email editor.
WhatsApp Business Integration:
This is a premium feature that sets Brevo apart from many basic email tools. Official, template-based messaging for commerce and support is a powerful modern touchpoint.
Shared Inbox & Live Chat:
Bring all customer conversations from chat, email, and messaging apps into a single team inbox. Assign chats, use saved replies, and ensure no message falls through the cracks.
Marketing Automation Workflows:

The visual workflow builder lets you create complex, multi-step automations triggered by any event (signup, purchase, page visit). You can branch paths based on user behavior, combining email, SMS, and WhatsApp steps.
Landing Pages & Forms:
Create dedicated pages to capture leads or promote offers without needing a separate website builder. These forms feed directly into your contact lists and automation workflows.
Your Action Plan: Get Started with These Best Ways
Don’t just read about Brevo—experience it. Here’s your simple action plan:
Sign up for the free account.
Spend 30 minutes importing a small test list and building a welcome email using the drag-and-drop editor. (
Start your free Brevo account today
Create one automated workflow.
Set up a simple 3-step automation: a welcome email, a follow-up with your top resource 2 days later, and an SMS check-in after 5 days.
Explore the reporting.
After sending, dive into the analytics. See who opened, who clicked, and which links performed best. Use this data to inform your next segment.
The platform’s interface is clean and intuitive, but if you’re looking for a structured walkthrough, our
step-by-step Brevo tutorial
breaks it down perfectly.

Why Choose Brevo Over Other Marketing Platforms?
Brevo Use Cases for Different Business Types
Frequently Asked Questions
Q: Is the free plan really free forever, or is it a trial?

A: The free plan is genuinely free forever, with no credit card required. It has daily sending limits (300 emails/day) and excludes advanced features like marketing automation, but it’s a fully functional starting point that never expires.
Q: Can I use Brevo for transactional emails like password resets or purchase receipts?

A: Absolutely. Brevo is excellent for transactional emails. You can use dedicated templates and the API to ensure critical, trigger-based messages are delivered reliably, and they’re tracked alongside your marketing emails.
Q: How does Brevo handle contact limits and pricing?

A: Pricing is based on the number of emails you send per month, not the number of contacts you store. This is a huge advantage, as you can build a large list without being penalized, and only pay for the volume you actually send. The Starter plan starts at $8.08/month for 5,000 emails.
Q: I’m migrating from another platform. Is it difficult?
A: Brevo offers import tools for contacts and can often help with migrating automation templates. Their support team is known for being responsive, which makes the transition smoother than expected.
My pick:
Try it free Brevo
